description: Primary key. Matches user_id in session_events. One row per user.
Behavioral segment. Stable across all user sessions.
Subscription tier. basic 35%, standard 40%, premium 25%.
Actual monthly charge. base_price + bundle_add × discount_mult.
1 if subscription is part of a telco/partner bundle. 30% of users.
1 if user has active promotional discount. 20% of users.
Months since subscription start. Uniform(1,60).
NEW v4.0. Lifetime value to date. Derived: monthly_price × tenure_months × payment_reliability_discount. Enables high-value persona prioritization for CX investment.

User's primary viewing device. Assigned at user level; session device_type adheres 70% to this field (Fix USR-01 — previously sessions used segment-level preference causing 34% mismatch between profile and session devices).
Second device. NULL for ~35% of users (single-device). Always different from primary_device.
range_or_values: mobile | tablet | smart_tv | laptop | desktop | NULL (~35%)

description: Screen size of primary device. Derived: smart_tv→large; mobile→small/medium.
1 if user habitually casts. smart_tv primary: 40%. Others: 5%. Note: session-level casting_usage_flag is always 0 when device_type=smart_tv.
User country. Drives geo_availability check against content_catalogue — users in countries not in a title's geo_availability get content_unavailable_flag=1 with reason=geo_block.
range_or_values: US | IN | UK | ZA | NG | KE | GH | BR | AU | CA

description: City within geo_country. Market-specific pool.

Internet service provider. Static per user (you don't change your ISP session to session). Market-specific: ZA→Vodacom/MTN/Telkom; NG→MTN/Airtel/Glo. Relevant for bundle partnership analysis. Note: session-level network_type has been moved to session_events.

NEW v4.0. Payment method. Market-calibrated: IN/NG/KE/GH skew mobile_money/carrier_billing; US/UK/AU skew card. Reveals payment friction persona dimension — mobile money users in emerging markets differ significantly from card users.
NEW v4.0. Plan change trajectory. Segment-calibrated: quick_churn skews downgraded; binge_heavy skews upgraded. Personas that previously downgraded are elevated churn risk.
NEW v4.0. 1 if user has used a promotional code. Correlated with discount_flag. Price-sensitive persona signal — identifies users acquired through discount who may churn at full price.

NEW v4.0. Customer lifecycle stage derived from behavioral fields. active: last_active_days<=7 AND binge_index>0.30. at_risk: recent churn_flag events in session history. reactivated: recent reactivation_flag events. churned: last_active_days>30 AND no recent reactivation.
NEW v4.0. Preferred channel for outreach. Segment-calibrated: binge_heavy skews email/push; quick_churn skews sms. Informs CX action routing for persona interventions.
NEW v4.0. 1 if user has opted in to push notifications. binge_heavy: 70%. quick_churn: 25%. Defines reachability for push-based win-back campaigns in twin simulations.

NEW v4.0. SPARSE — NULL for ~40% of users (no email engagement data available). Email open rate when present. Segment-calibrated: completion_obsessed mean ~55%; quick_churn mean ~12%. Strong signal for persona responsiveness to outreach.
NEW v4.0. SPARSE — NULL when email_open_rate is NULL. Email click-through rate. Always <= email_open_rate. Finer grain than open rate — measures intent, not just curiosity.
Derived composite health metric [0,1]. Formula: recency_score*0.35 + completion_score*0.30 + payment_score*0.20 - ticket_penalty. Low score = at-risk. High score = healthy engaged user.

NEW v5.0. SPARSE — NULL for ~40% of users (no campaign interaction history). Historical outreach response rate. Segment-calibrated: completion_obsessed ~40%, quick_churn ~8%. Defines 'marketing-receptive' vs 'dark' persona dimension. Synthetic values are segment-distributed priors; real causal value requires campaign system integration.

NEW v5.0. Most recent A/B test group assignment. not_tested(50%), treatment(25%), control(25%). A treatment user who did not churn is causally different from an untested user. Synthetic values are decorative — real causal value requires A/B test assignment log integration. Twin engine should treat as high-quality only when sourced from real data.

NEW v5.0. SPARSE — NULL for ~90% of users (non-purchasers). Pay-per-view event purchase count. Defines superfan / high-willingness-to-pay persona dimension that viewing behaviour alone cannot replicate. REQUIRES commerce data pipeline in production.

NEW v5.0. SPARSE — NULL for ~93% of users. Fan merchandise buyer. Strong co-occurrence with ppv_purchase_count — combination defines the superfan archetype. REQUIRES commerce data pipeline in production.

Mode of genre across all user sessions. Computed from session history, not independently sampled. Tie-break uses Python Counter.most_common insertion order (deterministic for fixed seed). Use fav_genre_confidence to assess signal strength.
range_or_values: Mode of genre column across user sessions

description: NEW v5.0. Fraction of sessions on the winning genre. 1.0 = all sessions same genre (strong preference). ~0.20 with 5 genres tied = essentially noise. Filter fav_genre_confidence > 0.4 before treating fav_genre as a reliable persona dimension.
Fraction of sessions with Sports genre. Session-history derived. Falls back to segment prior if no sports sessions.
Fraction of consecutive session pairs with gap < 24h. Denominator = n_sessions - 1 (not n_gaps). Single-session users return 0.0 explicitly. Computed from real session timestamps.
Mean inter-session gap in days from real timestamps.

Days since most recent session. Computed as (window_end_date - last_session_timestamp).days — NOT the final inter-session gap. A user active yesterday shows 1, not the gap between their last two sessions.
Payment failure count. SPARSE — NULL for ~88% of users. NULL ≠ zero failures — means absent from records.
Support ticket count. SPARSE — NULL for ~82% of users. All three ticket fields null together.
Issue types from tickets. NULL when ticket_count NULL. Segment-weighted: quick_churn→billing+login; binge_heavy→buffering.
Average ticket resolution hours. NULL when ticket_count NULL.
